prompt_text: Explain slope to a struggling 12-year-old in exactly 4 sentences.
model_a_answer: Slope is the steepness of a line, like how fast a ball rolls down a hill. It tells you how much the line goes up for every one unit you move forward. If the line goes up more than it goes down, the slope is positive; if it goes down, it's negative. A slope of zero means the line is flat and doesn't go up or down at all.
model_b_answer: Slope is a number that tells you how steep a line is, which is the same as the number of units you go up for every unit you move forward. It is calculated by dividing the total rise (going up) by the total run (going right). For example, if you climb 2 feet for every 1 foot you walk, your slope is 2. If the slope is 0, the line is flat and goes straight up or down without ever going up or down.
